name: frontend-api-client-with-jwt
description: A conceptual skill for building an API client in Next.js that handles JWT tokens
capabilities: Implement centralized API communication layer with JWT token attachment
Handle automatic token refresh before expiration
Parse and respond to authentication and authorization errors
Create type-safe API call patterns for TypeScript applications
Build request/response interceptors for cross-cutting concerns
author: Claude
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation describing JWT API client patterns for Next.js. No executable code, scripts, or network capabilities are present. Purely a conceptual guide for developers. All static findings are false positives from keyword detection in documentation - there is no code to execute, no network requests to make, and no credentials to exfiltrate.
valueStatement: Managing JWT authentication in API clients is complex and error-prone. This skill provides a framework for implementing secure, centralized API communication with automatic token refresh, error handling, and consistent response parsing in Next.js applications.
userTitle: Build API clients with JWT handling in Next.js
limitations: Does not include pre-built API client code or libraries
Requires manual implementation based on conceptual guidance
Does not cover backend JWT validation or signing

useCases.title: Next.js API Client Architecture
useCases.description: Design a centralized API client for Next.js applications with secure JWT token handling and automatic refresh.

useCases.title: Authentication Flow Integration
useCases.description: Implement seamless authentication flows with token refresh across client and server components.

useCases.title: React Hook API Patterns
useCases.description: Build custom hooks for API communication that handle JWT tokens and errors consistently.

promptTemplates.title: Basic API Client Setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a new API client with JWT
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an API client in Next.js that automatically attaches JWT tokens to requests and handles token refresh on 401 errors.

promptTemplates.title: Token Refresh Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement automatic token refresh in a Next.js application before JWT expiration to prevent request failures.

promptTemplates.title: Error Response Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Build error response parsing for an API client that identifies 401 and 403 errors and redirects users appropriately.

promptTemplates.title: Request Interceptors
promptTemplates.prompt: Create request interceptors for a Next.js API client that add authorization headers and log outgoing requests.

bestPractices: Store JWT tokens securely and validate them before making API requests to prevent unnecessary calls
Implement token refresh proactively before expiration rather than reactively after 401 errors
Use HTTPS for all API communications to protect tokens during transmission
antiPatterns: Storing JWT tokens in localStorage without XSS protection exposes tokens to script injection attacks
Making API calls without error handling causes silent failures and poor user experience
Implementing token refresh without coordination can cause race conditions with concurrent requests

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Next.js App Router and Pages Router?
faq.answer: Yes, the concepts apply to both Next.js App Router and Pages Router with context-specific implementations.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of concurrent API requests supported?
faq.answer: There is no fixed limit. The framework coordinates token refresh across requests without blocking.

faq.question: How does this integrate with existing authentication providers?
faq.answer: This skill provides patterns for any JWT-based auth. Integrate with your existing token storage and refresh endpoints.

faq.question: Are tokens stored securely in this implementation?
faq.answer: The skill describes secure patterns. Actual implementation depends on storage choice like cookies or secure storage.

faq.question: What should I do if token refresh fails repeatedly?
faq.answer: Redirect users to login after refresh failure. Clear stored tokens and provide clear error messaging.

faq.question: How does this compare to using Axios interceptors?
faq.answer: The skill provides similar interceptor patterns. Implementation can use fetch, Axios, or any HTTP client library.
